archive.subject: Rachana Sharir
keyConcepts: Femoral Triangle Location: Situated in the femoral region/groin directly below the inguinal ligament.
Anatomical Boundaries: Lateral boundary is formed by the medial border of the Sartorius muscle; medial boundary is formed by the medial border of the Adductor Longus muscle.
Roof Structures: Composed of the skin, superficial fascia, and deep fascia (fascia lata).
Major Contents: Includes the femoral nerve, femoral artery, femoral vein, and deep inguinal lymph nodes.

archive.transcript: To aaj hum padhenge apne Rachana Sharir ka third most important topic that is Srotas Sharir. To Srotas hote kya hain? These are the channels present in our body jo alag alag cheezon ke transport aur movement mein help karta hai. Ye chapter aapke exams mein seven marks ka aata hai aur jo topics is chapter ke liye aapke syllabus mein diye gaye hai wo hain iski definition, types of Srotas, number of Srotas and Srotas mool and its applied aspect. To ab hum padhenge Srotas ke bare mein. Ab jaise maine aapko bataya Srotas kya hote hain? Channels. Sabse pehle hum iski dekhenge nirukti ki Srotas word kaise bana hai. Shru gato dhatu se Srotas word ki nirukti hui hai. Ab iska matlab hota hai movement of pran, anna, ras, rakta jo bhi dhatuye hai, mal, in sabki movement kis mein hoti hai? Srotas mein. Next hai vyutpati, sravanat srotasi. Charak Samhita Sutrasthan 38. To is mein kya bataya gaya hai? Sravan karma wale ko hum Srotas kehte hai. Ab sravan ka word meaning kya hai? Secretion, oozing and transport. That means jisme bhi secretion, transport, movement ka karma hoga, use hum Srotas kahenge. Ab hum iski ek bahut important cheez padhenge that is paribhasha, means definition. To iske liye hum pehle iski puri Hindi ache se samjhenge and learn karenge jisse aage hum iska shlok easily derive karke learn kar lenge. So that aapko dono Hindi and shlok ek sath learn ho jayega. To Srotas kya hote hai? Mool chhidra se deham mein prasrit. Ab iska kya matlab hua? Mool chhidra, means main hole, chhidra hota hai hole. To main hole se puri deham mein that is hamari puri body mein prasrit, means fela hua. To ek main kisi jagah se puri hamari body mein jo fela hua hota hai, aage kya likha hai? Ras aadi ka vahan, means ras aadi jo hamari dhatue hai, pran, anna, jo hamare mal hai, in sab ka jo vahan karta hai, that is transport karta hai, use Srotas kehte hai and ye sira and dhamni, that is veins and arteries se alag hai, use different hai. Mool chhidra, means main jagah se puri deham mein jo prasrit, fela hua hai aur ras aadi ka vahan karta hai, transport karta hai, use hum Srotas kahenge and ye sira and dhamni se alag hai. To agar aapko ye meaning pura isi order mein bilkul learn ho gaya hai to hum iska shlok bhi easily learn kar lenge. Shlok mein kya likha hai? Moolat kha adantar, ab moolat kha adantar ka matlab hai mool chhidra dehe prasritam, ye wahi matlab hua dehe mein jo prasrit hai, twapi wahi means jisme sari cheezein bahti hai, vahan karti hai ya Srotas tat iti, means use Srotas hum vikayam kehenge. Aage hai sira dhamni varjitam, means ye sira and dhamni se alag hai. To aise pura shlok ho gaya moolat kha adantaram dehe prasritam twapi wahi yat Srotas tat iti vikayam sira dhamni varjitam. Sushrut Samhita Sharirsthan 9/25. Aage hum iske do chote points padhenge that is prakar. To jitne bhi hamari body mein murti wale that is hard organs hai, utne hi hamari body mein Srotas hote hain. Aage swarup ki ye Srotas hote kaise hain, to like dhatu means is channels mein jo bhi dhatu transport hogi, Srotas bhi usi ke jaise gol, mote, sukshm aur aakriti mein lambe honge. Ab hum karenge iske types, to Srotas aage two types mein divided hai, that is bahirmukh and antarmukh. Bahirmukh ka matlab aap iske word se hi samajh lijiye bahirmukh that is external jiski opening hame bahar dikhegi, antarmukh ho gaya internal jiski openings andar honi, to bahirmukh ke under total 9 Srotas aate hai plus 3 in females. Ye sare bahirmukh Srotas aapko learn karna easy hote hai as ye aap khud bhi dekh sakte hai. To pehle hum dekhenge apne first nine Srotas ismein hai do kan, ears, do aankhein, eyes, do naak, nose, ek mukh, mouth, ek guda, anus and ek shishan that is male genital. And plus 3 jo females mein present hote hai, wo hai do stan that is breast and ek artava that is cervix. To ye the sare ke sare bahirmukh Srotas. Hamari jo second type hai that is antarmukh, sare acharyon ne alag alag counts bataye hain, to acharya Sushrut ne antarmukh Srotas ko yogvahi Srotas bhi kaha hai, means jisme dravya move karte hai. Acharya Sushrut ne kaha hai ki antarmukh Srotas 11 in pairs hamari body mein present hai, then acharya Charak ne kaha hai 13 in pairs. Next Asthang Hriday ne adrist Srotas kaha hai inhe, and inhone bhi 13 in pairs kaha hai. Ab yaha par humne jo 11, 13, 13 pade, ye aapko yaad rakhna hai ki ye sare in pairs hai, means jo bhi hum Srotas padhenge wo hamari body mein 2 present hote hai. Hum in antarmukh Srotas ke naam padhenge, sabse pehle according to Acharya Charak to inhone kaha hai, first pranvaha Srotas that is jisme pran, oxygen behti hai, second udakvaha Srotas that is jisme jal, pani behta hai, water. Next annavaha Srotas that is jisme food behta hai. Next hai 7 dhatue that is hamari jo 7 dhatue hai ras, rakta, mansa, meda, asthi, majja, shukra, in sabke Srotas hote hain and next last hai 3 mal, that is mutra-vaha Srotas, swedvaha Srotas and purishvaha Srotas. Jo hamare 3 mal hai, urine, sweat and feces, in sabke 3 Srotas, to aise total 13 Srotas hai but they all are in pairs. To humein ye kaise yaad rakhne hai, sabse pehle 3 cheezein aayengi that is air, water and food. Ye aapko pata hi hai hamare liye sabse important, to pranvaha Srotas, udakvaha Srotas and then annavaha Srotas, then hamare 7 dhatue and 3 mal. Ab next hai according to Acharya Sushrut, hamare 11 pairs of Srotas to Acharya Sushrut ne upar diye huye 13 Srotas mein se jo changes kiye hai, wo hai 7 dhatuo mein and 3 mal mein. Unhone 7 dhatuo mein se 3 dhatu ke Srotas nahi mane hai and ek mal ka Srotas nahi mana hai jisme do dhatuye hai asthi and majjavaha Srotas and ek mal hai swedvaha Srotas and unhone jis ek Srotas ko mana hai wo hai artvavaha Srotas, ab ye jo excluded hamare Srotas hai ye hume bhool jate hai mostly to iske liye hamari magic box ki trick hai AIMS. To A se hoga asthi, then next M se hoga majja, asthi ka hi...

archive.transcript: Harih Om. Today we will recite the classical lineage of Ayurveda Descent, known as Ayurvedavatarana. Please open your notebooks as requested. The lineage flows from Brahma, who recollected the science of life (Brahma Smrtva Ayushovedam), to Prajapati (Daksha Prajapati). From Prajapati, the knowledge was passed to the Ashvini Kumaras, who then taught Lord Indra. Sage Bharadvaja descended to Indra's abode to obtain this eternal wisdom for the welfare of mankind. Bharadvaja then imparted it to Atreya Punarvasu. Atreya taught his six disciples: Agnivesha, Bhela, Jatukarna, Parasara, Harita, and Ksarapani. Agnivesha compiled the Agnivesha Tantra, which was later redacted by Sage Charaka, becoming the Charaka Samhita. Alongside this, we will review the basic Sanskrit grammar inflections (Vibhakti) for the masculine noun 'Vaidya' (Vaidyah, Vaidyau, Vaidyah). Write these declensions down carefully.

archive.transcript: Hari Om students. Today we will recite and understand the historical line of Ayurveda Avataran, which is the descent of Ayurveda. Please open your books. The traditional lineage begins: Brahma smritva ayushovedam... Brahma first remembered Ayurveda, then passed it to Prajapati, who taught it to the Ashwini Kumaras. From Ashwini Kumaras, the knowledge went to Indra. Then, Indra transmitted this sacred science to Sage Bharadvaja. Bharadvaja then taught the Atreya Punarvasu and other sages, which eventually led to Agnivesha, Charaka, and the classical treatises we study today. Look at the Sanskrit vibhaktis used in these slokas to represent the direction of transfer. Write down this exact transfer line in your notebooks right now. Thank you.

archive.subject: Kriya Sharir
archive.transcript: Srotas is a very fundamental concept in Kriya Sharir. What is Srotas? Sravanat srotamsi. The channels through which material or nutrients flow or secrete are called Srotas. Every Dhatu, every Upadhatu, Mala, and Prana has its own Srotas. Charaka mentions thirteen Srotas, while Sushruta mentions eleven pairs, which means twenty-two Srotas because Sushruta excludes the Dhatus like Asthi, Majja, and Sweda from his main list of Srotas, although they are present. Today we will focus on the Mulasthana, the root of these Srotas. For example, Pranavaha Srotas has its Mula in Hridaya and Mahasrotas. Annavaha Srotas has its root in Amashaya and Vamaparshva. Udakavaha Srotas, which carries water and fluids, has its Mulasthana at Talu and Kloma. Now coming to Dhatu-vaha Srotas. Rasavaha Srotas, which carries the nutrient plasma, has its root in Hridaya and the Dashadhamanis. Raktavaha Srotas, carrying blood, has its root in Yakrit and Pliha, that is liver and spleen. Srotomula is clinically very important because any injury or trauma to the Srotomula will lead to severe complications or death. We must also understand Srotodushti Samanya Lakshanas. What happens when Srotas are vitiated? There are four main types of Srotodushti: Atipravritti, Sanga, Siranam Granthi, and Vimargagamana. Atipravritti is excessive flow. Sanga is obstruction or stagnation. Siragranthi is formation of nodes or dilatation of channels. Vimargagamana is movement of fluids in the wrong direction, like vomiting or reverse peristalsis. Please remember these, as these concepts are basic and critical.

archive.transcript: Chayovriddhihi sadosheshu prasweshu vrihi ke bhashu. Its activation occurs, if you look at the properties, how is Vata activated? Because of the similar properties, properties are increased in your body. So, Vata will undergo chaya. Sadosha... its own location, it is during the initiation. Then, prasweshu vrihi ke bhashu. What are the factors responsible for initiation? The person feels aversion towards those factors. If the person is having Kaphakopa, they will have aversion towards cold items. That is Chayavastha. What are the factors responsible for the increase of Kapha Dosha? To those factors, the person is having aversion. What are the factors responsible for Kapha increase? What type of food? Guru ahara, Atisnigda ahara, Sheeta ahara, more Sheeta ahara. When you are exposed to cold climate, all these are having Kapha properties. So, what happens? These factors are increasing Kapha Dosha in your body, so you are having aversion towards these increasing factors. Chayavastha, Kopavastha, Shamavastha. Ashtanga Hridaya, first chapter. Chaya, Kopa, and Shama avastha, three stages for the Kriya Kala. It is a very important shloka. Next, another important shloka from Ashtanga Hridaya, Sutrasthana 12th chapter. Chaya prakopa prashamaha vayorgreeshmadhishu, varshadhishu pitta. What is written here? Chaya, Prakopa, and Prashama according to season. How are they telling? Vayorgreeshmadhishu... three seasons. Vata Chaya is in Grishma. In Grishma, Varsha, and Sharad, Chaya, Kopa, and Shama of Vata Dosha occur. Varshadhishu pittasya... Varsha, Sharad, and Hemanta. Pitta Chaya, Kopa, and Shama. Then, Shishiradhishu Kaphasya... Chaya, Kopa, and Shama of Kapha occur in Shishira, Vasanta, and Grishma. So, Vasanta is the Kopa avastha of Kapha. What is the treatment for Kapha Kopa? Vamana chikitsa. In Shishira what happens? Pitta Kopa occurs... and for Pitta Prakopa in Sharad, we do Raktamokshana and Virechana treatment. Like that you have to study. In each season, you have to eliminate the particular prakopavastha dosha from your body so you will be healthy. Acharya is telling basic scientific reasons. In another shloka, they are telling why seasons are like this, what is the reason? See, ushnaiva rukhshadhya vayuhu kurvanti sanchayam, sheetena kopa ushnena shamam, snigdhadhibhihi. It is a very important shloka. The previous shloka's continuation is this one. When does Chaya of Vata occur? What Gunas are combining together? Chaya occurs when properties of Vata like Ruksha etc. combine with Ushna Guna. Ruksha Guna + Ushna Guna leads to Vata Chaya. The same properties of Vata, when combined with Sheeta Guna... in the presence of Sheeta, Vata Prakopa occurs. The same properties of Vata combine with Sheeta Guna to cause Vata Kopa. Then, ushnena shamam snigdhadhibhihi. When does Vata Shama occur? When opposite properties of Vata, like Snigdha and Ushna Guna, are combined. It will reduce the increased Vata. Vata Shama occurs. The same you have to apply for your clinical practice. Whenever Vata is increased, you have to follow opposite treatment, choosing opposite properties in medicines and food. Snigdha Guna and Ushna Guna will reduce Vata. Next, Pitta Dosha. How does Pitta Kriya Kala occur? Sheetena... Chaya of Pitta occurs when Sheeta Guna is combining with the native properties of Pitta Dosha like Teekshna and Ushna. What happens? Chaya of Pitta. The same properties, when combined with Ushna Guna, cause Pitta Kopa. Pitta Kopa occurs in Sharad. And Shama occurs when Manda Guna of Kapha or other opposite properties are combined. It will reduce the increased Pitta. Next is Kapha. Chaya of Kapha occurs in Shishira, Kopa in Vasanta, and Prashama in Grishma. So, what are the factors responsible?

Seasonal Variations of Vata, Pitta, and Kapha: Vata accumulates in Grishma, excites in Varsha, pacifies in Sharad. Pitta accumulates in Varsha, excites in Sharad, pacifies in Hemanta. Kapha accumulates in Shishira, excites in Vasanta, pacifies in Grishma.
Guna-Based Explanations: The combining actions of Gunas (e.g., Ruksha + Ushna causing Vata Chaya, whereas Ruksha + Sheeta causes Vata Prakopa) as the logical scientific basis of seasonal changes.

Clinical Application & Shodhana: Understanding seasonal kopa is critical for timing clinical interventions, such as Vamana in Vasanta ritu to treat Kapha Prakopa, and Virechana or Raktamokshana in Sharad ritu to treat Pitta Prakopa.

archive.transcript: NCISM syllabus states that Abhava is a crucial padartha to understand. Some people might ask, what is the utility of non-existence? Abhava means non-existence. In clinical diagnosis, how does the void of a symptom serve to diagnose health? It is simple. The absence of jwara, which is fever, indicates the existence of swasthya, or health. Pragabhava, Pradhvamsabhava, Atyantabhava, and Anyonyabhava are the four types of Abhava. If a patient does not present with any symptoms of a disease, it is through Abhava pramana or the understanding of non-existence of pathology that we establish the state of health. This is how the void of a symptom serves as a valid logical construct for diagnosing health. Write this down as it is very important for your exams. The NCISM syllabus requires you to understand both the philosophical and clinical aspects of Abhava.

keyConcepts: Vaidya as the supreme entity among the Chikitsa Chatuspada, capable of managing treatments even with deficits in other limbs.
Yadriccha Siddhi vs. Systematic Cure: Accidental recovery under an unqualified doctor compared to structured recovery under a competent physician.
Pranaabhisara Vaidya: The savior of life who possesses complete theoretical knowledge (Shastra) and practical experience (Karma Darshana).
Rajaarha Bhishak: The Royal Physician characterized by four-fold diagnostic and therapeutic knowledge (Hetu, Linga, Prashamana, and Apunarbhava).
Shastra-Salilam Analogy: Scriptures, like water, adopt the qualities (virtues or defects) of the intellect (container) that holds them.

archive.transcript: Upasthata, Roga, Chatuspada. Shastra includes expert in the field. He can give treatment, right? Doctor. And even in the absence of Paricharaka or in the absence of... even if the medicine is not having the proper four qualities, still, if Vaidya is expert, he can treat in a proper way to the patient. Shastrakami, as he is expert in the field, is having the strong knowledge, who can be in charge of quality of doctor. Yogtavid, he can plan treatment, he can utilize all the available resources in a proper way to treat the disease of the patient. That is my key point. And if a Vaidya is not having enough knowledge, or if there is... if the Vaidya doesn't have the good qualities of Vaidya, then sometimes medicine may get choked or sometimes the patient may die, or the disease may get severe. Is it? So the curing of disease, even if a Vaidya is inefficient, or if the Vaidya is not having the proper knowledge, if still the disease is cured, then it may be accidental. Yadriccha siddhi, accidental. Luck of treatment or luck of the doctor. Is it? That is accidental cure. So it is same like... everything is accidental, don't think that it is cured by the efficiency of the Vaidya. And then, the entire thing is explained about the Vaidya who is efficient or who can cure the disease. What type of Vaidya can cure the disease, or what we can call the Vaidya who is efficient to cure the disease. It is explained in Shloka number 18. Such Vaidya is called Pranaabhisara Vaidya. Pranaabhisara Vaidya, who can restore the life of the patient. Who is having capacity? Vaidya who has complete knowledge of Shastra, and can perform the karma with efficiency. He can use his capacity to bring back the life of the person, that is why such Vaidya is called Pranaabhisara Vaidya. Shashtre artha vijnaane pravrittau karma darshane. Next, continuing to the next Shloka, Shloka number 19, as it is written: 'Hetau lingae prashamane roginaam apunarbhave, jnaatam chaturvidham yasya sa raajaarho bhishaktama.' Rajaarha Bhishak, royal physician. What does it mean? Who is able to treat? One who knows: 1. Hetu, 2. Linga, 3. Prashamana, 4. Roganam Apunarbhave. These are four important things that he needs to know. One who can identify the nidana, diagnose properly by observing the lakshanas, and can treat the disease accordingly, without recurrence of the disease, then he is considered as a royal physician (Rajaarha Bhishak). To justify this, another Shloka is explained: 'Yathashastram shastram hi salilam... gunadosha pravrittaye... paatrabekshi... chikitsartham vishodhayet'. What is the meaning of Shastra? Scripture. Salilam means water. Salilam, water. Water, if kept in a vessel, takes the quality of the vessel. Similarly, all these scriptures have their own guna and dosha, both positive and negative nature. How the guna and dosha of these are exhibited depends upon the vessel, meaning where it is present. Who is holding it. If the Shastra or any instrument or any weapon is in the right hand...

The clavicle is commonly known as the collar bone or beauty bone.
It is the only long bone in the human body that is positioned horizontally.
The clavicle consists of two surfaces (superior and inferior), two borders (anterior and posterior), and two ends (sternal and acromial).
Its anterior border is concave, whereas its posterior border is convex.

keyConcepts: Femoral Triangle Location: Situated in the femoral region/groin directly below the inguinal ligament.
Anatomical Boundaries: Lateral boundary is formed by the medial border of the Sartorius muscle; medial boundary is formed by the medial border of the Adductor Longus muscle.
Roof Structures: Composed of the skin, superficial fascia, and deep fascia (fascia lata).
Major Contents: Includes the femoral nerve, femoral artery, femoral vein, and deep inguinal lymph nodes.

Monday.transcript: To aaj hum padhenge apne Rachana Sharir ka third most important topic that is Srotas Sharir. To Srotas hote kya hain? These are the channels present in our body jo alag alag cheezon ke transport aur movement mein help karta hai. Ye chapter aapke exams mein seven marks ka aata hai aur jo topics is chapter ke liye aapke syllabus mein diye gaye hai wo hain iski definition, types of Srotas, number of Srotas and Srotas mool and its applied aspect. To ab hum padhenge Srotas ke bare mein. Ab jaise maine aapko bataya Srotas kya hote hain? Channels. Sabse pehle hum iski dekhenge nirukti ki Srotas word kaise bana hai. Shru gato dhatu se Srotas word ki nirukti hui hai. Ab iska matlab hota hai movement of pran, anna, ras, rakta jo bhi dhatuye hai, mal, in sabki movement kis mein hoti hai? Srotas mein. Next hai vyutpati, sravanat srotasi. Charak Samhita Sutrasthan 38. To is mein kya bataya gaya hai? Sravan karma wale ko hum Srotas kehte hai. Ab sravan ka word meaning kya hai? Secretion, oozing and transport. That means jisme bhi secretion, transport, movement ka karma hoga, use hum Srotas kahenge. Ab hum iski ek bahut important cheez padhenge that is paribhasha, means definition. To iske liye hum pehle iski puri Hindi ache se samjhenge and learn karenge jisse aage hum iska shlok easily derive karke learn kar lenge. So that aapko dono Hindi and shlok ek sath learn ho jayega. To Srotas kya hote hai? Mool chhidra se deham mein prasrit. Ab iska kya matlab hua? Mool chhidra, means main hole, chhidra hota hai hole. To main hole se puri deham mein that is hamari puri body mein prasrit, means fela hua. To ek main kisi jagah se puri hamari body mein jo fela hua hota hai, aage kya likha hai? Ras aadi ka vahan, means ras aadi jo hamari dhatue hai, pran, anna, jo hamare mal hai, in sab ka jo vahan karta hai, that is transport karta hai, use Srotas kehte hai and ye sira and dhamni, that is veins and arteries se alag hai, use different hai. Mool chhidra, means main jagah se puri deham mein jo prasrit, fela hua hai aur ras aadi ka vahan karta hai, transport karta hai, use hum Srotas kahenge and ye sira and dhamni se alag hai. To agar aapko ye meaning pura isi order mein bilkul learn ho gaya hai to hum iska shlok bhi easily learn kar lenge. Shlok mein kya likha hai? Moolat kha adantar, ab moolat kha adantar ka matlab hai mool chhidra dehe prasritam, ye wahi matlab hua dehe mein jo prasrit hai, twapi wahi means jisme sari cheezein bahti hai, vahan karti hai ya Srotas tat iti, means use Srotas hum vikayam kehenge. Aage hai sira dhamni varjitam, means ye sira and dhamni se alag hai. To aise pura shlok ho gaya moolat kha adantaram dehe prasritam twapi wahi yat Srotas tat iti vikayam sira dhamni varjitam. Sushrut Samhita Sharirsthan 9/25. Aage hum iske do chote points padhenge that is prakar. To jitne bhi hamari body mein murti wale that is hard organs hai, utne hi hamari body mein Srotas hote hain. Aage swarup ki ye Srotas hote kaise hain, to like dhatu means is channels mein jo bhi dhatu transport hogi, Srotas bhi usi ke jaise gol, mote, sukshm aur aakriti mein lambe honge. Ab hum karenge iske types, to Srotas aage two types mein divided hai, that is bahirmukh and antarmukh. Bahirmukh ka matlab aap iske word se hi samajh lijiye bahirmukh that is external jiski opening hame bahar dikhegi, antarmukh ho gaya internal jiski openings andar honi, to bahirmukh ke under total 9 Srotas aate hai plus 3 in females. Ye sare bahirmukh Srotas aapko learn karna easy hote hai as ye aap khud bhi dekh sakte hai. To pehle hum dekhenge apne first nine Srotas ismein hai do kan, ears, do aankhein, eyes, do naak, nose, ek mukh, mouth, ek guda, anus and ek shishan that is male genital. And plus 3 jo females mein present hote hai, wo hai do stan that is breast and ek artava that is cervix. To ye the sare ke sare bahirmukh Srotas. Hamari jo second type hai that is antarmukh, sare acharyon ne alag alag counts bataye hain, to acharya Sushrut ne antarmukh Srotas ko yogvahi Srotas bhi kaha hai, means jisme dravya move karte hai. Acharya Sushrut ne kaha hai ki antarmukh Srotas 11 in pairs hamari body mein present hai, then acharya Charak ne kaha hai 13 in pairs. Next Asthang Hriday ne adrist Srotas kaha hai inhe, and inhone bhi 13 in pairs kaha hai. Ab yaha par humne jo 11, 13, 13 pade, ye aapko yaad rakhna hai ki ye sare in pairs hai, means jo bhi hum Srotas padhenge wo hamari body mein 2 present hote hai. Hum in antarmukh Srotas ke naam padhenge, sabse pehle according to Acharya Charak to inhone kaha hai, first pranvaha Srotas that is jisme pran, oxygen behti hai, second udakvaha Srotas that is jisme jal, pani behta hai, water. Next annavaha Srotas that is jisme food behta hai. Next hai 7 dhatue that is hamari jo 7 dhatue hai ras, rakta, mansa, meda, asthi, majja, shukra, in sabke Srotas hote hain and next last hai 3 mal, that is mutra-vaha Srotas, swedvaha Srotas and purishvaha Srotas. Jo hamare 3 mal hai, urine, sweat and feces, in sabke 3 Srotas, to aise total 13 Srotas hai but they all are in pairs. To humein ye kaise yaad rakhne hai, sabse pehle 3 cheezein aayengi that is air, water and food. Ye aapko pata hi hai hamare liye sabse important, to pranvaha Srotas, udakvaha Srotas and then annavaha Srotas, then hamare 7 dhatue and 3 mal. Ab next hai according to Acharya Sushrut, hamare 11 pairs of Srotas to Acharya Sushrut ne upar diye huye 13 Srotas mein se jo changes kiye hai, wo hai 7 dhatuo mein and 3 mal mein. Unhone 7 dhatuo mein se 3 dhatu ke Srotas nahi mane hai and ek mal ka Srotas nahi mana hai jisme do dhatuye hai asthi and majjavaha Srotas and ek mal hai swedvaha Srotas and unhone jis ek Srotas ko mana hai wo hai artvavaha Srotas, ab ye jo excluded hamare Srotas hai ye hume bhool jate hai mostly to iske liye hamari magic box ki trick hai AIMS. To A se hoga asthi, then next M se hoga majja, asthi ka hi...

Friday.transcript: Chayovriddhihi sadosheshu prasweshu vrihi ke bhashu. Its activation occurs, if you look at the properties, how is Vata activated? Because of the similar properties, properties are increased in your body. So, Vata will undergo chaya. Sadosha... its own location, it is during the initiation. Then, prasweshu vrihi ke bhashu. What are the factors responsible for initiation? The person feels aversion towards those factors. If the person is having Kaphakopa, they will have aversion towards cold items. That is Chayavastha. What are the factors responsible for the increase of Kapha Dosha? To those factors, the person is having aversion. What are the factors responsible for Kapha increase? What type of food? Guru ahara, Atisnigda ahara, Sheeta ahara, more Sheeta ahara. When you are exposed to cold climate, all these are having Kapha properties. So, what happens? These factors are increasing Kapha Dosha in your body, so you are having aversion towards these increasing factors. Chayavastha, Kopavastha, Shamavastha. Ashtanga Hridaya, first chapter. Chaya, Kopa, and Shama avastha, three stages for the Kriya Kala. It is a very important shloka. Next, another important shloka from Ashtanga Hridaya, Sutrasthana 12th chapter. Chaya prakopa prashamaha vayorgreeshmadhishu, varshadhishu pitta. What is written here? Chaya, Prakopa, and Prashama according to season. How are they telling? Vayorgreeshmadhishu... three seasons. Vata Chaya is in Grishma. In Grishma, Varsha, and Sharad, Chaya, Kopa, and Shama of Vata Dosha occur. Varshadhishu pittasya... Varsha, Sharad, and Hemanta. Pitta Chaya, Kopa, and Shama. Then, Shishiradhishu Kaphasya... Chaya, Kopa, and Shama of Kapha occur in Shishira, Vasanta, and Grishma. So, Vasanta is the Kopa avastha of Kapha. What is the treatment for Kapha Kopa? Vamana chikitsa. In Shishira what happens? Pitta Kopa occurs... and for Pitta Prakopa in Sharad, we do Raktamokshana and Virechana treatment. Like that you have to study. In each season, you have to eliminate the particular prakopavastha dosha from your body so you will be healthy. Acharya is telling basic scientific reasons. In another shloka, they are telling why seasons are like this, what is the reason? See, ushnaiva rukhshadhya vayuhu kurvanti sanchayam, sheetena kopa ushnena shamam, snigdhadhibhihi. It is a very important shloka. The previous shloka's continuation is this one. When does Chaya of Vata occur? What Gunas are combining together? Chaya occurs when properties of Vata like Ruksha etc. combine with Ushna Guna. Ruksha Guna + Ushna Guna leads to Vata Chaya. The same properties of Vata, when combined with Sheeta Guna... in the presence of Sheeta, Vata Prakopa occurs. The same properties of Vata combine with Sheeta Guna to cause Vata Kopa. Chaya of Pitta occurs when Sheeta Guna is combining with the native properties of Pitta Dosha like Teekshna and Ushna. What happens? Chaya of Pitta. Wednesday.classNotes:
# Detailed Class Notes on Vyadhi Classification (Saptavidha Vyadhi Ayatana)

## Introduction to Vyadhi Classification
Vyadhi (disease) is a state of pain or discomfort caused by the vitiation of Doshas. To understand, diagnose, and treat diseases effectively, Ayurveda offers various classifications of Vyadhi based on their pathogenesis, origin, and prognosis.

## 1. Classification based on Dosha Involvement (Anubandhana)
Diseases are categorized according to the number of vitiated Doshas involved:
*   **Ekadoshaja (Kevala):** Involves a single Dosha (Vata, Pitta, or Kapha) without any combination.
*   **Dvidoshaja (Samsargaja):** Caused by the simultaneous vitiation and combination of two Doshas (Vata-Pitta, Pitta-Kapha, or Vata-Kapha).
*   **Tridoshaja (Sannipataja):** Caused by the vitiation and combination of all three Doshas (Vata-Pitta-Kapha).

## 2. Classification based on Origin (Nija vs. Agantuja)
*   **Nija Vyadhi:** Diseases arising from internal factors, i.e., the intrinsic vitiation of bodily Doshas (Vata, Pitta, Kapha) caused by improper diet and lifestyle.
*   **Agantuja Vyadhi:** Diseases initiated by external factors (abhighata - injury, visha - poison, bhuta - organisms, etc.) where Dosha vitiation occurs secondarily after the injury or affliction.

## 3. Sushruta's Saptavidha Vyadhi Ayatana (Seven-fold Classification)
Acharya Sushruta classifies all diseases into seven categories under three primary groups (Trividha Duhkha):

### Group A: Adhyatmika (Physical and Mental Diseases originating from within)
1.  **Adibala Pravritta (Hereditary/Genetic):** 
    *   Diseases caused by defects in the male sperm (Shukra) or female ovum (Shonita) at the time of conception.
    *   Examples: Kushtha (skin diseases), Arshas (hemorrhoids), Prameha (diabetes) passed from parents to offspring.
2.  **Janmabala Pravritta (Congenital):**
    *   Diseases arising due to improper conduct of the mother during pregnancy (Garbhini Aparadha) or dietary errors (Mithyahara-Vihara).
    *   Examples: Congenital blindness (Jatyandha), deafness (Badhirya), dwarfism (Vamanata).
3.  **Doshabala Pravritta (Humoral/Idiopathic):**
    *   Diseases caused directly by dietary and lifestyle indiscretion leading to immediate Dosha vitiation.
    *   These can be further subdivided into Amashayauttha (originating from the stomach) and Pakvashayauttha (originating from the colon).

### Group B: Adhibhautika (Diseases originating from external physical environment)
4.  **Sanghatabala Pravritta (Traumatic):**
    *   Diseases caused by external trauma, physical injury, or attacks.
    *   Examples: Injuries from weapons, falls, wild animal bites, or contact with toxic substances.

### Group C: Adhidaivika (Diseases caused by natural, providential, or supernatural forces)
5.  **Kalabala Pravritta (Seasonal/Chronological):**
    *   Diseases caused by seasonal variations, extreme cold, heat, rain, or failure to adapt to seasonal transitions (Ritu-Sandhi).
6.  **Daivabala Pravritta (Providential/Supernatural):**
    *   Diseases caused by invisible forces, divine wrath, contagious epidemics (Janapadodhvansa), or lightning strikes.
7.  **Svabhavabala Pravritta (Natural/Physiological states):**
    *   Natural physiological processes that bring distress but are inevitable.
    *   Examples: Natural hunger (Kshut), thirst (Pipasa), sleep (Nidra), aging (Jara), and death (Mrityu).
Wednesday.subject: Samhita Adyayan
Wednesday.transcript: Samsarga is the combination of two doshas. Sannipata is the combination of three doshas. Vyadhi can be classified into Ekadoshaja, Dvidoshaja or Samsargaja, and Tridoshaja or Sannipataja. Then we have Nija and Agantuja classification of Vyadhi. Nija vyadhis are caused by internal dosha imbalance. Agantuja are caused by external factors like injury, poison, etc. Further, Sushruta Samhita describes Saptavidha Vyadhi Ayatana. These are classified into three main groups: Adhyatmika, Adhibhautika, and Adhidaivika. Adhyatmika contains Adibala pravritta, Janmabala pravritta, and Doshabala pravritta. Adhibhautika contains Sanghatabala pravritta. Adhidaivika contains Kalabala pravritta, Daivabala pravritta, and Svabhavabala pravritta. Adibala pravritta are hereditary diseases due to defects in Shukra and Shonita. Janmabala pravritta are congenital, due to maternal errors during pregnancy. Doshabala pravritta are due to dietary and lifestyle errors causing dosha vitiation.

Tuesday.subject: Kriya Sharir
Tuesday.transcript: Srotas is a very fundamental concept in Kriya Sharir. What is Srotas? Sravanat srotamsi. The channels through which material or nutrients flow or secrete are called Srotas. Every Dhatu, every Upadhatu, Mala, and Prana has its own Srotas. Charaka mentions thirteen Srotas, while Sushruta mentions eleven pairs, which means twenty-two Srotas because Sushruta excludes the Dhatus like Asthi, Majja, and Sweda from his main list of Srotas, although they are present. Today we will focus on the Mulasthana, the root of these Srotas. For example, Pranavaha Srotas has its Mula in Hridaya and Mahasrotas. Annavaha Srotas has its root in Amashaya and Vamaparshva. Udakavaha Srotas, which carries water and fluids, has its Mulasthana at Talu and Kloma. Now coming to Dhatu-vaha Srotas. Rasavaha Srotas, which carries the nutrient plasma, has its root in Hridaya and the Dashadhamanis. Raktavaha Srotas, carrying blood, has its root in Yakrit and Pliha, that is liver and spleen. Srotomula is clinically very important because any injury or trauma to the Srotomula will lead to severe complications or death. We must also understand Srotodushti Samanya Lakshanas. What happens when Srotas are vitiated? There are four main types of Srotodushti: Atipravritti, Sanga, Siranam Granthi, and Vimargagamana. Atipravritti is excessive flow. Sanga is obstruction or stagnation. Siragranthi is formation of nodes or dilatation of channels. Vimargagamana is movement of fluids in the wrong direction, like vomiting or reverse peristalsis. Please remember these, as these concepts are basic and critical.
